proposed_hypotheses.text: The outcome pfs_months is approximately right-skewed with substantial spread (range 0 to >15 months), so subsequent feature-vs-PFS analyses can use mean differences and OLS regression on the natural scale.

Among the 33 features, some are binary (0/1) plausibly representing treatment assignments or mutation indicators, while others are continuous (lab values, demographics); the binary features will be the primary candidates for 'treatment' variables for heterogeneity analyses.

analyses.code: df['pfs_months'].describe()
analyses.result_summary: PFS distribution: n=50000, mean=4.31, std=2.29, min=0.00, 25%=2.72, median=4.21, 75%=5.74, max=16.25 months. Roughly symmetric on the natural scale; OLS is appropriate.

analyses.code: for c in df.columns: ... check unique values
analyses.result_summary: 15 binary features (feature_031, _014, _006, _023, _011, _022, _016, _028, _002, _024, _010, _012, _018, _020, _027), one 3-level (feature_001 with levels 0,1,2), and 17 continuous features. Patient ID column has unique values per row. No missing values.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each binary feature has a non-zero univariate association with mean pfs_months; specifically, feature_014 and feature_023/feature_006 are associated with shorter PFS, while at least one binary feature (a candidate treatment) is associated with longer PFS.

analyses.code: for f in bin_feats: stats.ttest_ind(df[f==1].pfs_months, df[f==0].pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Largest negative associations: feature_014 (mean PFS 3.70 vs 5.06, diff=-1.35, p~0); feature_023 (4.12 vs 4.45, diff=-0.33, p=2e-58); feature_006 (4.11 vs 4.42, diff=-0.31, p=1e-49); feature_022 (4.09 vs 4.32, diff=-0.23, p=3e-7). Largest POSITIVE association: feature_027 (mean PFS 5.09 vs 4.12, diff=+0.97, p=2e-216) — strongest treatment-like main effect. feature_011 also positive but tiny (+0.22, p=7e-4).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65) is strongly and monotonically associated with pfs_months on a positive direction (higher feature_015 -> longer PFS).

Among the other continuous features, feature_019, feature_025, and feature_005 are statistically associated with PFS at p<0.001, while many lab values (feature_007, feature_032, feature_017, feature_008, etc.) have negligible association.

analyses.code: stats.spearmanr(df.feature_015, df.pfs_months); df.groupby(qcut(feature_015,5)).pfs_months.mean()
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 vs PFS: Spearman rho=+0.785, p~0. Quintile means rise monotonically: 1.86 -> 3.33 -> 4.30 -> 5.27 -> 6.83 months. Decile means span 1.33 to 7.45 months. feature_015 dominates the prognostic signal.

analyses.code: for f in cont_feats: spearmanr(df[f], df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: feature_025: rho=-0.120 (p=6e-160); feature_019: rho=+0.103 (p=4e-117); feature_005: rho=-0.047 (p=1e-25). All other continuous features have |rho|<0.02 and p>0.001 except feature_030 (rho=-0.014, p=0.002). feature_021, feature_032, feature_017, feature_008, feature_004, feature_007, feature_033, feature_009 are essentially uncorrelated with PFS marginally.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (3-level: 0,1,2) shows monotonically declining mean PFS from level 0 to level 2 (consistent with a stage- or performance-status-like ordinal severity indicator).

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_001').pfs_months.mean(); stats.f_oneway(level0,level1,level2)
analyses.result_summary: feature_001=0 (n=17592): mean PFS 5.27; feature_001=1 (n=24971): 4.05; feature_001=2 (n=7437): 2.91. ANOVA F=3528.9, p~0. Each unit increase in feature_001 corresponds to ~1.18 months shorter PFS — a major ordinal prognostic factor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When all 33 features are entered jointly into an OLS model for pfs_months, feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_027 (positive), feature_006, feature_023, feature_022, feature_005, and feature_025 remain significant independent predictors, while many lab values drop to non-significance — indicating substantial collinearity and redundancy among the noise features.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(pfs_months, all_features).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Joint OLS on all 33 features (R^2=0.86): significant independent effects per unit are feature_015 +0.176 (t=454), feature_001 -1.171 (t=-206), feature_014 -1.362 (t=-176), feature_027 +0.943 (t=98), feature_019 +0.469 (t=61), feature_025 -0.076 (t=-76), feature_023 -0.354 (t=-44), feature_006 -0.287 (t=-36), feature_005 -0.005 (t=-21), feature_022 -0.357 (t=-19), feature_021 -4e-4 (t=-9). Non-significant (p>0.05): feature_031, _011, _016, _028, _002, _032, _017, _024, _010, _012, _018, _020, _003, _030, _008, _004, _013, _026, _029, _007, _033, _009. These appear to be true noise/uncorrelated lab features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 functions as the principal therapeutic intervention in this cohort: it is binary, has ~20% prevalence, and shows the largest positive marginal effect on PFS of any feature; therefore subsequent treatment-effect heterogeneity analyses should focus on feature_027 as the treatment.

analyses.code: df.feature_027.mean(); ttest_ind treated vs untreated; OLS coef in joint model
analyses.result_summary: feature_027 prevalence 20.0% (n=10022 treated, 39978 untreated). Unadjusted mean PFS 5.09 vs 4.12 (diff +0.97, p=2e-216). Adjusted joint OLS coef +0.94, t=98 — largest positive effect among binary candidates. Treated as the candidate 'treatment' for downstream heterogeneity work.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_027 is preserved across feature_001 (severity) strata — i.e., the additive treatment effect is roughly +1 month regardless of feature_001 level, indicating no important interaction between feature_027 and the severity ordinal.
Similarly the PFS benefit of feature_027 is roughly preserved across feature_014 strata.

analyses.code: stratified ttest of feature_027 within each feature_001 level
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_001=0: f027 effect +0.93 (p=5e-76). Within feature_001=1: +1.00 (p=4e-122). Within feature_001=2: +0.90 (p=3e-34). Effect is essentially constant across severity. Joint OLS interaction f027:feature_001 coef=-0.022, p=0.067 (NS). Hypothesis supported.

analyses.code: stratified ttest of feature_027 within each feature_014 level
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_014=0: f027 effect +0.94 (p=6e-95). Within feature_014=1: +1.01 (p=4e-140). Joint OLS interaction f027:feature_014 coef=+0.057, p=2e-4 — statistically significant but trivially small in magnitude. Hypothesis essentially supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 modifies the PFS effect of feature_027: the +1 month average treatment effect of feature_027 is concentrated in patients with feature_006=0 and is essentially abolished when feature_006=1 (consistent with feature_006 being a resistance marker, e.g., a driver mutation).

analyses.code: stratified ttest of f027 within each f006 level; OLS f027*f006 interaction
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_006=0: f027 effect +1.48 mo (n_treated=6524, p=4e-274). Within feature_006=1: f027 effect +0.03 mo (n_treated=3498, p=0.52). Joint OLS interaction f027:f006 coef=-1.45, p=4e-168. STRONG modifier: feature_006=1 abolishes treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 also modifies the PFS effect of feature_027 in the same direction: feature_023=1 abolishes the treatment benefit, whereas feature_023=0 patients gain ~1.7 months of PFS from feature_027.

analyses.code: stratified ttest of f027 within each f023 level; OLS f027*f023 interaction
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_023=0: f027 effect +1.66 mo (n_treated=5816, p=6e-293). Within feature_023=1: f027 effect +0.03 mo (n_treated=4206, p=0.46). Joint OLS interaction f027:f023 coef=-1.63, p=8e-226. STRONG modifier; feature_023=1 abolishes treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 (rare ~4.5% binary) likewise modifies feature_027 effect: feature_022=1 patients receive no PFS benefit (or trend toward harm) from feature_027, whereas feature_022=0 patients show full +1 month benefit.

feature_023 and feature_022 are mutually exclusive (never co-occur), consistent with biologically alternative driver mutations (e.g., one being KRAS-like and the other BRAF-like in colorectal cancer).

analyses.code: stratified ttest f027 within f022 levels; OLS f027*f022 interaction
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_022=0: f027 effect +1.03 mo (n_treated=9554, p=5e-226). Within feature_022=1: f027 effect -0.16 mo (n_treated=468, p=0.13). Joint OLS interaction f027:f022 coef=-1.18, p=6e-23. feature_022=1 abolishes (and slightly reverses) treatment benefit.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df.feature_023, df.feature_022)
analyses.result_summary: Cross-tab: feature_023=0/feature_022=0: 26830; feature_023=0/feature_022=1: 2272; feature_023=1/feature_022=0: 20898; feature_023=1/feature_022=1: 0. Mutual exclusivity is exact. Likewise feature_011 (1506 patients) is mutually exclusive with both feature_023 and feature_022.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 (right-skewed continuous, plausible tumor-marker-like distribution with values from 0.02 to 777) acts primarily as a TREATMENT-EFFECT MODIFIER of feature_027 rather than as a baseline prognostic factor: higher feature_005 reduces or abolishes the PFS benefit of feature_027 in a dose-dependent manner, but feature_005 has little or no main effect on PFS once the f027 interaction is included.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027*feature_005 (+ adjustments)
analyses.result_summary: OLS f027:feature_005 interaction coef=-0.025 per unit feature_005, p=9e-58. Within wild-type subgroup (f006=0,f023=0,f022=0): feature_005 main coef=-3e-5 p=0.95 (effectively zero), but f027:feature_005 interaction=-0.063 per unit, p=4e-220 (massive). Stratified by feature_005 quintiles: f027 effect drops monotonically (+1.84, +1.75, +1.23, +0.04, +0.01 mo across quintiles). Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining a 'wild-type-like' subgroup as feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0, the PFS benefit of feature_027 within this subgroup is much larger (~2.7 months) than the marginal +1 month, while in the complementary 'mutated' subgroup feature_027 confers essentially no PFS benefit.

analyses.code: df['wt'] = (f006==0)&(f023==0)&(f022==0); ttest_ind treatment effect within each
analyses.result_summary: Wild-type-like subgroup (n=17372, 34.7% of cohort): f027 effect +2.76 mo (n_treated=3461, mean PFS 6.88 vs 4.12, p~0). Complement (n=32628): f027 effect +0.03 mo (mean 4.15 vs 4.12, p=0.29). Treatment benefit of f027 is ENTIRELY concentrated in the wild-type subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Combining the wild-type-like subgroup with low feature_005 (<= median 4.44) yields an even larger and more concentrated PFS benefit of feature_027 (~5 months), defining the optimally responsive subgroup.

analyses.code: subgroup: f006=0 & f023=0 & f022=0 & feature_005 <= median
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup (n=8656, 17.3% of cohort): f027 effect +5.03 mo (n_treated=1784, mean PFS 9.12 vs 4.08, p~0). Outside this subgroup (n=41344): f027 effect +0.09 mo (p=4e-4, trivially significant due to large n but tiny magnitude). Within-subgroup PFS gain of >5 months is the largest treatment effect identified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the wild-type-like subgroup, the PFS gain from feature_027 declines linearly with feature_005, with a treatment effect of ~+3 mo at feature_005 near 0 falling toward 0 around feature_005 ~= 50 (no obvious threshold; continuous dose-response).

analyses.code: Threshold sweep on feature_005 within wild-type subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within wild-type subgroup, f027 effect by feature_005 cap: <=1: +4.96; <=2: +5.04; <=3: +5.03; <=5: +5.01; <=7.5: +4.02; <=10: +3.61; <=15: +3.26; <=20: +3.07; <=30: +2.92; <=50: +2.83. The benefit is preserved up to feature_005 ~ 5 then declines. Within-subgroup OLS adjusted f027 coef=+3.00 with f027:feature_005 slope=-0.063/unit; treatment benefit reaches 0 around feature_005 = 47.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner CATE estimate (separate OLS in treated and untreated arms, predicted difference) shows substantial heterogeneity (CATE ranging from -18 to +2.8), and a depth-4 regression tree on the CATE recovers exactly the same subgroup structure: feature_023, feature_006, feature_022, and feature_005 are the dominant splitters.

analyses.code: T-learner OLS CATE then DecisionTreeRegressor(max_depth=4) on CATE
analyses.result_summary: Tree splits: top split feature_023 (<=0.5 vs >0.5); next split feature_006 (<=0.5 vs >0.5); next split feature_022 (<=0.5 vs >0.5); next split feature_005 thresholds. Best leaf: feature_023=0 & feature_006=0 & feature_022=0 & feature_005<=37.9 -> predicted CATE +2.34. Worst leaf: feature_023=1 & feature_006=1 & feature_005>28 -> CATE -2.06. Tree exactly confirms the multivariate subgroup story; no additional important modifiers were found.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Systematic interaction screening of feature_027 with every other feature one-at-a-time identifies feature_023, feature_006, feature_005, and feature_022 as the only modifiers with overwhelming statistical evidence (p<1e-20); feature_011 shows a smaller but real interaction (p~1e-11), while no other feature reaches strong evidence.

analyses.code: for f in all_feats: ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027 * f'); rank by interaction p
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p-values for feature_027 x feature, top 8: feature_023 (coef -1.63, p=8e-226), feature_006 (-1.45, p=4e-168), feature_005 (-0.025, p=9e-58), feature_022 (-1.18, p=6e-23), feature_011 (+0.99, p=1e-11), feature_007 (+0.013, p=0.11), feature_019 (-0.077, p=0.13), feature_013 (-0.12, p=0.13). Only the first five are credible modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 is a positive modifier of feature_027 because feature_011 is mutually exclusive with feature_023 and feature_022, so feature_011=1 patients are guaranteed to be in (or near) the wild-type subgroup; once we condition on the wild-type subgroup, feature_011 adds essentially nothing to the treatment effect.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest f027 by f011 within wild-type subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within wild-type subgroup, f027 effect with feature_011=0: +2.75 mo (n_treated=3264). With feature_011=1: +2.91 mo (n_treated=197). Difference is small (+0.16 mo) and no interaction beyond what's explained by mutation status. feature_011 is essentially redundant with the wild-type definition for treatment-effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the continuous features, no other feature beyond feature_005 shows a credible treatment-effect interaction with feature_027 once we account for multiple testing — interactions with feature_013, feature_019, feature_017, feature_029, etc., all have p>0.01 and small magnitudes, indicating no additional continuous modifiers.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 * (all continuous features)
analyses.result_summary: Continuous-feature interactions with feature_027 (sorted by p): feature_013 (-0.12, p=0.013), feature_019 (-0.074, p=0.016), feature_017 (+0.016, p=0.026), feature_029 (-0.006, p=0.043). All others p>0.05. None survive Bonferroni correction across ~16 tests, and the magnitudes are negligible. Only feature_005 (already in model) is a real continuous modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 acts as a strong PROGNOSTIC factor (large positive linear effect on PFS) but NOT as a treatment-effect modifier of feature_027 (interaction with f027 is non-significant).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 * feature_015 (+ adjustments)
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 main effect +0.176 mo per unit (t=454). f027:feature_015 interaction coef=-0.0017 per unit, p=0.24 — non-significant. feature_015 contributes to baseline PFS but does not alter the size of the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 is a negative prognostic factor (~-1.4 mo per unit) and its effect is largely additive with feature_001 (no important interaction), and adjusting for it does not change the treatment-effect modifier picture for feature_027.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_014 * C(feature_001)
analyses.result_summary: feature_014 main coef=-1.40. Interaction feature_014:C(feature_001)[T.1]=+0.06, p=0.17 (NS); feature_014:C(feature_001)[T.2]=+0.13, p=0.02 (small, not clinically meaningful). Effects of feature_014 and feature_001 are essentially additive. Adjusting for them does not change the dominant treatment-effect modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive 'wild-type AND low feature_005' subgroup, no other binary feature substantially modifies the feature_027 treatment effect — i.e., the four-way subgroup definition is essentially complete and other binary features (feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024, feature_011, feature_016, feature_028, feature_031) have no additional explanatory power for treatment heterogeneity.

analyses.code: Within {f006=0,f023=0,f022=0,f005<=median}, ttest mean PFS by each binary feature
analyses.result_summary: Within the responsive subgroup (n=8656), mean-PFS differences by each remaining binary feature (controlling for treatment indirectly): feature_031 +0.04 (p=0.54); feature_014 -1.41 (p=9e-112, but pure prognosis, not treatment modification); feature_011 +0.12 (p=0.43); feature_016 +0.08 (p=0.58); feature_028 -0.06 (p=0.75); feature_024 -0.14 (p=0.04); feature_010 +0.04 (p=0.51); feature_012 +0.02 (p=0.79); feature_018 +0.02 (p=0.88); feature_020 -0.02 (p=0.86). No additional treatment-effect modifiers; only feature_014 stays prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An adjusted multivariable model that includes the strongest prognostic factors (feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_019, feature_025, feature_021) plus feature_027 with its interactions {feature_006, feature_023, feature_022, feature_005} captures essentially all of the explainable variation in PFS attributable to treatment-effect heterogeneity, with R^2 jumping from 0.86 (main effects only) to 0.90 (with treatment interactions).

analyses.code: Compare R^2 of OLS main-effects vs OLS with f027 interactions
analyses.result_summary: Main-effects model R^2 = 0.8586. Model adding feature_027 * (feature_006 + feature_023 + feature_022 + feature_005) interactions: R^2 = 0.9023. Improvement of +0.044 in R^2 entirely attributable to the treatment-effect-heterogeneity terms. Final treatment block: f027 main +2.51, f027:f006 -1.48, f027:f023 -1.77, f027:f022 -1.76, f027:f005 -0.025; f005 main effect ~0 (p=0.97).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients in the unfavorable mutation strata (any of feature_006=1, feature_023=1, or feature_022=1) gain ESSENTIALLY ZERO PFS benefit from feature_027 (point estimate +0.03 mo, 95% CI roughly +/- 0.05 mo), so within current evidence feature_027 should not be expected to help these patients.

analyses.code: ttest f027 in mutated subgroup (f006=1 OR f023=1 OR f022=1)
analyses.result_summary: Mutated subgroup (n=32628): f027 effect +0.030 mo (n_treated=6561, mean 4.15 vs 4.12, p=0.29). Treatment effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero despite n>30000. Confirms that feature_027 is futile in this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete best-supported subgroup definition for feature_027 efficacy is: feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005 below ~5 (or more generally, the lower the feature_005, the larger the gain). Within this 4-predicate subgroup, feature_027 confers an absolute PFS benefit of roughly +5 months. Each of the four predicates is REQUIRED — removing any one of feature_006=0, feature_023=0, feature_022=0 abolishes the treatment effect, and high feature_005 substantially attenuates it even when all three mutations are negative.

analyses.code: Stratified analyses combining all four predicates
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup f006=0 & f023=0 & f022=0 & feature_005<=median: f027 effect +5.03 mo (p~0). With each single predicate removed: dropping the f006=0 requirement reduces effect to ~0; dropping f023=0 reduces effect to ~0; dropping f022=0 has small impact (because f022 is rare); dropping the f005<= median condition reduces effect to +2.76 mo (still large). All four predicates are jointly important and the subgroup is internally consistent across stratified, regression, and tree-based analyses.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS: feature_027 is the only treatment in the dataset with a clinically meaningful PFS benefit, and that benefit is concentrated in patients who are negative for ALL THREE of feature_006, feature_023, feature_022 AND who have low feature_005 (e.g., feature_005 <= 5). In this subgroup the PFS gain is approximately +5 months. In the complementary subgroup (any of feature_006=1, feature_023=1, feature_022=1, OR high feature_005), feature_027 confers essentially no PFS benefit. No other binary or continuous feature in the dataset appears to be a treatment, and no other feature meaningfully modifies the feature_027 treatment effect once these four are accounted for.

PROGNOSTIC SUMMARY: Independent of treatment, longer PFS is predicted by higher feature_015 (~+0.18 mo per unit, dominant), higher feature_019 (~+0.46 mo per unit), lower feature_001 (~-1.18 mo per level), absence of feature_014 (~-1.36 mo if present), and lower feature_025 (~-0.08 mo per unit). These five plus feature_027/feature_006/feature_023/feature_022/feature_005 explain ~90% of variance in PFS.

analyses.code: Final consolidated subgroup analysis described in iteration 24; corroborated by tree (iteration 15)
analyses.result_summary: BEST SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: Treatment=feature_027; Outcome=pfs_months (direction: HIGHER PFS = better); Subgroup predicates: feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<=5 (or more generally, feature_005 small). Within-subgroup feature_027 effect: +5.03 months PFS (n=8656; treated n=1784; p<1e-300). Outside-subgroup effect ~+0.1 mo. The three mutation predicates are required because each is independently sufficient to abolish benefit; feature_005<=5 is required to maximize benefit but partial benefit (~+2.8 mo) persists across the wild-type group at any feature_005 level.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all major predictors + treatment interactions
analyses.result_summary: Final R^2 of full model = 0.9023. Coefficients: feature_015 +0.176/u, feature_019 +0.46/u, feature_001 -1.18/level, feature_014 -1.36, feature_025 -0.076/u, feature_021 -4e-4/u, plus the treatment block above. All non-listed features have coefficients indistinguishable from zero in this final model and represent uninformative noise variables.
